events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes control wiring in the same raceway as power wiring for VFDs and motor controls. This is a common NEC violation. NEC 430.130 requires that conductors for motor control circuits be separated from power conductors, and more broadly, NEC 300.3(C) addresses conductors of different systems in the same raceway. Control wiring mixed with power wiring can cause interference and is a non-compliant installation. The work has been tested/inspected, so this defect was either missed or not corrected.

Key issues:
- Control wiring in same raceway as power wiring - this is non-compliant per NEC
- NEC 300.3(C) and 430.130 - separation of control and power conductors
- This could cause electromagnetic interference, signal integrity issues, and safety concerns
- The work passed through testing/inspection but this defect exists

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: medium-high (interference, potential for malfunction)
Work stage: tested/inspected
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: ~90% (tested/inspected but needs rework)

Let me write theJSON.
